items.title: Claude's Fable 5 just solved an 87-year-old math problem, and it matters for bitcoin
items.description: Claude Fabel 5 disproved the Jacobian conjecture over the weekend, landing days after the capabilities of China's Kimi AI became the biggest force moving bitcoin markets.

items.title: Galaxy sets up $5 million fund to help shield Bitcoin against quantum computing threats
items.description: The crypto financial services firm is funding developers to strengthen the blockchain's security before quantum computers become powerful enough to steal billions in crypto.

items.title: Russia’s parliament passes crypto market law with $3,800 annual cap for retail investors
items.description: While citizens still cannot use Bitcoin to buy groceries, the Kremlin is officially letting companies use digital tokens to bypass sanctions and other global trade hurdles.

items.title: Augustus raises $180 million to build a clearing bank for the AI and stablecoin era
items.description: The firm, freshly valued at $1 billion, aims to replace legacy correspondent banking with always-on infrastructure that connects traditional payment systems and stablecoins.

items.title: MoneyGram's CEO says blockchain works best when customers don't know it's there
items.description: In an interview with CoinDesk, MoneyGram CEO Anthony Soohoo said that their blockchain strategy has evolved from early experimentation into a broader effort to modernize the company's global payments infrastructure.

items.title: Jack Mallers steps down as XXI Capital CEO as Tether's plans to merge three bitcoin firms falls
items.description: The proposed three-way merger between Twenty One Capital, Mallers' Strike, and Elektron Energy has been abandoned.

items.title: Bitcoin rally has broad-based support as institutions, whales, options traders pile in
items.description: Your day-ahead look for July 21, 2026

items.title: Clarity odds jump to 43% on Polymarket after unverified reports Trump agreed to ethics deal
items.description: Bettors moved the crypto market structure bill to a 43% chance of passing this year, up from a record low last week, though no bill text has appeared.

items.title: BIS warns stablecoins could weaken capital controls in emerging markets
items.description: Researchers found dollar-backed stablecoins are less affected by capital controls than traditional bank deposits, raising new questions about monetary sovereignty in emerging markets.

items.title: MEXC adds Bittensor TAO staking for its global user base
items.description: The integration with validator Yuma gives millions of MEXC users access to Bittensor, an AI-focused blockchain whose ecosystem now spans 128 specialized subnets.

items.title: Bitcoin nears seven-week high as stocks ignore Iran strikes, Trump 10% tariff plans
items.description: Bitcoin and crypto joined US stocks in brushing off US-Iran war escalation and the threat of fresh trade tariffs before the end of the month.

items.title: CoinShares debuts Bitcoin mining ETF in Europe entrance
items.description: The UCITS ETF, CoinShares’ first in Europe, began trading on Deutsche Börse Xetra, tracking a rules-based index of publicly listed BTC miners.

items.title: Robinhood-backed DEX Arcus expands with tokenized assets, perps
items.description: Arcus, built by the dYdX team, adds tokenized stocks and perpetual futures on Robinhood Chain as platforms compete to bring traditional assets onchain.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Morpho launches fixed-rate lending protocol on Base
items.description: Morpho Midnight introduces fixed rates and defined maturities alongside Morpho Blue’s variable-rate lending markets.

items.title: Tether-backed Twenty One, Strike merger plan scrapped: Bloomberg
items.description: Strike will remain a standalone company after the proposed three-way merger was scrapped, while Twenty One Capital and Elektron continue discussions, Bloomberg reported.

items.title: SpaceX sets earnings date, triggering first lock-up expiration for millions of shares
items.description: SpaceX's stock gained on Tuesday and looked to snap a seven-day losing streak after announcing Aug. 4 as its maiden earnings report

items.title: Greer hints new Trump tariffs coming on dozens of countries: 'Expect action soon'
items.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer's comments the Trump administration could impose new tariffs just as other duties are set to expire.

items.title: IRS chief Frank Bisignano laughs off JPMorgan spying allegations
items.description: Bisignano denied allegations The Wall Street Journal reported that he surveilled his colleagues while working at JPMorgan more than a decade ago.

items.title: OpenAI, Anthropic boost lobbying as legacy tech and defense spending slips
items.description: The two AI developers spent a combined $3.17 million in the second quarter, up 23% from the first three months of 2026.

items.title: U.S. strikes Iran and Houthis threaten Saudi Arabia shipping as mediators push 10-day ceasefire
items.description: The latest cycle of strikes comes amid reports that regional mediators have presented Washington and Tehran with a proposal for a 10-day ceasefire.

items.title: Nvidia details its next-generation Vera CPU for AI, setting up challenge to AMD and Intel
items.description: Nvidia released new information about its data center CPU, called Vera, that prospective customers need to fully evaluate the chip.

items.title: Google expands Gemini lineup with cheaper models and new Mythos rival
items.description: Google is expanding Gemini with cheaper, more efficient models and a new cybersecurity offering as it tries to close product gaps and compete on cost.

items.title: Bessent says U.S. could sanction China over AI model 'theft'
items.description: Chinese open-weight models are gaining steam against leading offerings from American companies like OpenAI and Anthropic.

items.description: Donald Trump’s tariffs have taught us an important lesson in the value of retaliation

items.title: Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly over ‘deliberately false’ weight-loss drug ads

items.title: Biotech deals surge to record as fear of missing out grips Big Pharma
items.description: Prospect of patents expiring on lucrative drugs prompts drugmakers to hunt for next blockbuster medicines

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AQriP4CmlJs! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Here’s how to time your retirement to keep your healthcare costs down
items.description: Plus, more ways to lower how much you pay out of pocket for medical care in retirement.

items.title: An oil lifeline is under threat, and markets have yet to price in the growing crisis
items.description: The Houthis escalated threats on Tuesday, risking millions of barrels of Saudi oil that had been reaching energy markets despite the U.S.-Iran war.

items.title: SpaceX’s stock looks to snap brutal losing streak as it heads for one of its best days yet
items.description: Plus, investors just found out when insiders can start dumping their shares.

items.title: Microsoft has 3 secret weapons that could drive its stock 50% higher, analyst says
items.description: Wall Street is misjudging Microsoft’s AI strategy, according to Morgan Stanley.

items.title: These signs suggest investors should give the software sector a second chance
items.description: Software stocks and loans have started to find their footing in July, suggesting it may be time for investors to forgive and forget.

items.title: Pro stock pickers cannot beat simple math — war in the Middle East is proving it
items.description: Active stock pickers almost always lose against the broad market. They can’t beat simple math.

items.title: Novo Nordisk’s lawsuit against Eli Lilly over ads highlights intense competition in GLP-1 market
items.description: The Danish company Novo Nordisk says U.S.-based Lilly isn’t including the most up-to-date clinical information about its drugs.

items.title: Think this stock market is in a bubble? These earnings numbers say otherwise.
items.description: Stay with the AI trade and overweight technology stocks, according to one earnings expert.

items.title: India police detain opposition leader Rahul Gandhi as tensions rise
items.description: Opposition leader defiant as police remove him from sit-in outside Prime Minister Modi’s residence

items.title: The bear hug is an increasingly sensible way to play UK M&A
items.description: Negotiations are being played out in public — no bad thing given boards and investors may see things differently

items.title: Monetary Policy Radar preview: ECB’s July meeting
items.description: The FT’s guide on what to look out for in Thursday’s session

items.title: IEA warns of risk to energy supplies from escalation of Iran conflict
items.description: Intergovernmental agency says ‘no room for complacency’ and points to tightness in refined products

items.title: Andy Burnham’s cabinet: who’s in, who’s out?
items.description: Plus: policy announcements and how to fund them

items.title: The Yahoo Boys — the true stories of four Nigerian online love scammers
items.description: A quest to expose fraudsters in Lagos led Carlos Barragán to a criminal racket fuelled by poverty — and by western loneliness

items.title: Turkish opposition leader to create breakaway party to take on Erdoğan
items.description: Move by Özgür Özel could threaten longtime Turkish leader at next election scheduled for 2028

items.title: Burnham backs away from costly increase to income tax allowance
items.description: New prime minister had explored raising £12,570 personal allowance before taking office on Monday

items.title: Oil Buyers Battered by the Iran War Energy Crisis Race to Build Buffers
items.description: As rising fuel costs clobber their economies, the Philippines and other developing countries in Asia are pushing to build strategic petroleum reserves to cushion against future shocks.

items.title: Trump Escalates Canada Tariffs as Mark Carney Holds Firm
items.description: The president’s new tariffs on a top U.S. trading partner aim at gaining concessions, but the Canadian government, and the public, have been bracing for the escalation.

items.title: Will the U.S. and China Build Walls Around A.I.?
items.description: Rapid advancements in Chinese artificial intelligence models reportedly have Washington and Beijing considering new ways to protect their technology sectors.

items.title: Why States Are Making It Harder to Save Money With Solar Panels
items.description: The changes are costing homeowners money. But they also reflect a changing grid — and the need for home batteries.

items.title: Trump to Impose 50% Tariff on Many Canadian Goods
items.description: The administration will use an untested legal provision to put significant duties on Canadian exports, reigniting a clash with one of America’s biggest trading partners.

items.title: Andy Burnham, New UK Prime Minister, Names John Healey as Top Finance Official
items.description: As chancellor, John Healey, who quit as defense secretary in the former government, will fill one of the most consequential posts, overseeing fiscal and economic policy.

items.title: Judge Temporarily Pauses Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
items.description: The delay is intended to give the court time to consider a lawsuit that argues the $111 billion merger violates antitrust laws.

items.title: Trump Administration Sought Phone Records of Times Journalists and Their Relatives
items.description: The government is aggressively seeking the confidential sources of Times reporters who wrote about the president’s new Air Force One. The Times has asked a judge to quash the subpoenas.
